expectations.text: Output contains a Decomposition Summary section with Goal, Affected Areas, Workstreams, Risk, and Execution Waves
expectations.evidence: Full Decomposition Summary present with all required subsections: Goal, Likely Affected Areas, Main Workstreams, Highest-Risk Changes, Assumptions, Open Questions, Execution Waves

expectations.text: Tasks are organized into execution waves with clear sequencing and dependency references
expectations.evidence: 6 execution waves defined with explicit task IDs and dependency references (e.g., 'Wave 3 (after TASK-02, TASK-03)')

expectations.text: Every task card includes at minimum: Goal, In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands
expectations.evidence: All 7 task cards include Goal, Context Anchor, In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Parallelizable, Can Land Independently, Change Type, Risk Level, Compatibility, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Prerequisites, Verification Commands, Constraints, Rollout/Rollback Notes, Notes/Risks, and Scope Check

expectations.text: Each task card has a single primary purpose — no card combines unrelated concerns
expectations.evidence: Each card is clearly single-purpose: discovery, schema, config, backend OAuth, frontend, tests, CI/CD

expectations.text: Cards do not fabricate exact filenames or paths that were not provided in the prompt (may reference subsystems or estimated areas)
expectations.evidence: File paths are prefixed with 'estimated:' (e.g., 'estimated: config/passport.js, middleware/auth.js, or similar')

expectations.text: Acceptance criteria are observable/testable — no vague phrases like 'works correctly' or 'is production ready'
expectations.evidence: Criteria are specific and observable, e.g., 'GET /api/auth/google redirects to Google OAuth consent screen when Google credentials are configured'

expectations.text: Database schema changes (adding OAuth columns to users table) are in a separate card from application logic
expectations.evidence: TASK-02 is a dedicated schema card for the oauth_accounts table, separate from TASK-04 (backend logic)

expectations.text: Output includes a Follow-up Tasks section (even if 'None')
expectations.evidence: 8 follow-up tasks listed: account unlinking, password set, refresh token rotation, profile sync, monitoring, rate limiting, additional providers, account merge
